title: Epipactis latina
intro: Epipactis latifolia, commonly known as the broad-leaved helleborine, is a perennial orchid species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The health benefits of Epipactis latifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Epipactis latifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and fever, as well as to promote wound healing and skin regeneration.
taxonomy: Epipactis latina belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a species of orchid and is classified as a terrestrial orchid.
